codeReview.summary: The feature added 4 unit tests for the pre-existing CostGuard struct in crates/swell-core/src/types.rs. All tests pass and verify: (1) add_cost updates spent correctly, (2) is_warning_threshold returns true at 75%, (3) is_hard_stop returns true at 100%, (4) warning is true while hard stop is false between 75-100%.

issues.description: Test filter inconsistency: `cargo test -p swell-core -- CostGuard` returns 0 tests, but `cargo test -p swell-core` runs all 4 CostGuard tests successfully. The tests are in a nested `#[cfg(test)]` module. This may confuse future debugging but does not affect functionality since the tests do run and pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker reported pre-existing clippy issues in swell-memory (unused_assignments in pattern_learning.rs:781, unused updated_count variable) and swell-memory/src/recall.rs:89 (method name from_str may confuse std::str::FromStr). These are documented in the handoff but not in AGENTS.md under Known Pre-Existing Issues. AGENTS.md currently lists 'None currently identified' for Known Pre-Existing Issues.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues lists two non-blocking clippy issues not documented in AGENTS.md

summary: Feature passes review. The CostGuard struct (budget_limit, spent, warning_threshold at 75%, hard_stop_threshold at 100%) was already implemented. This feature added 4 comprehensive unit tests covering all expected behavior. All 37 tests in swell-core pass. Workspace builds successfully. One non-blocking observation: pre-existing clippy warnings in swell-memory are documented in handoff but not in AGENTS.md.
